dag_page.preset_create_roadmap_prefix: Please create a learning roadmap for me. My goal is
dag_page.preset_add_node_prefix: Please add a new node to the roadmap. The content is
dag_page.no_course_selected: ⚠️ No course selected — go back to the course list and pick one

node_page.chat_empty:
Describe your learning goal
and I'll generate materials for you
node_page.preset_gen_theory_prefix: Based on the current node's three foundation blueprints, please generate theory material. Requirements:
node_page.preset_gen_theory_desc: A full explanation for first-pass learning
node_page.preset_gen_practice_prefix: Based on the current node's Practice & Exercise Blueprint, please generate practice exercises. Requirements:
node_page.preset_gen_review_prefix: I've finished this node. Please generate a Feynman review checklist. Requirements:
node_page.preset_gen_outline_prefix: Please generate the three foundation blueprints for the current node: v1 Learning Blueprint, v2 Practice & Exercise Blueprint, and v3 Review & Deepening Blueprint
node_page.preset_gen_topic_prefix: I want to create a focused topic deep dive for this node. First list possible directions from the three foundation blueprints, including KCs, exercise types, misconceptions, or application scenarios, then I'll pick one.
node_page.preset_gen_topic_desc: Manually extend a KC or scenario
node_page.preset_external_reference_index_prefix: Please generate an External Reference Index for the current node and save it to the Theory folder by default. Include textbooks/PDFs, official docs, open courses/videos, papers/surveys, open notebooks, simulations/visualizations, datasets, and real cases. For each item, include a link or copyable search query, use case, quality/risk note, and whether it should be imported locally. Generate only this external-reference index file; do not generate full standard theory material.
node_page.preset_artifact_concept_cards_prefix: Create a Concept Cards Markdown file in the Theory folder for the most important concepts in the current node. Each card should include: one-sentence definition, why it matters, minimal example, counterexample/boundary, common misconception, and self-check question. Create only this concept-card file; do not generate a full standard theory material.
node_page.preset_artifact_case_study_desc: Use concrete scenarios to connect ideas
node_page.preset_artifact_case_study_prefix: Create a Case Study Markdown file in the Theory folder with 2-3 typical or realistic application scenarios for the current node. Each case should include: situation, related KCs, reasoning steps, key decisions, common mistakes, and transfer question. Create only this case-study file; do not generate a full standard theory material.
node_page.preset_artifact_examples_prefix: Create an Examples and Nonexamples Markdown file in the Theory folder. For the core concepts in this node, include positive examples, counterexamples, boundary cases, and confusing near-misses. For each group, explain the judgment rule, exposed misconception, and one self-check question. Create only this file; do not generate a full standard theory material.
node_page.preset_artifact_decision_tree_prefix: Create a Decision Tree Markdown file in the Theory folder using a Mermaid flowchart plus short notes. It should help me distinguish the most confusing concepts or situations in this node. Keep node labels short, then add usage notes and 3 test cases after the diagram. Create only this decision-tree file; do not generate a full standard theory material.
node_page.preset_artifact_derivation_desc: Skeleton for key formulas and theorems
node_page.preset_artifact_derivation_prefix: Create a Derivation and Proof Skeleton Markdown file in the Theory folder for the most important formula or theorem in this node. Include assumptions, goal, key lemmas, derivation steps, intuition for each step, common sticking points, and one fill-in exercise. Create only this file; do not generate a full standard theory material.
node_page.preset_artifact_checklist_prefix: Create a Self-check Checklist Markdown file in the Feynman Review folder. It should list the behavioral evidence a learner should show after learning this node, common error checks, oral explanation prompts, and next-step reinforcement suggestions. Create only this checklist file; do not generate a full standard review material.
node_page.preset_artifact_code_lab_desc: Runnable code blocks and observation tasks
node_page.preset_artifact_code_lab_prefix: Create a Code Lab Markdown file in the Practice folder with a small runnable experiment for the current node. Include goal, prerequisites, code block, sample input/output, observation tasks, parameters to modify, and reflection questions. Create only this code-lab file; do not generate a full standard practice material.
node_page.preset_artifact_csv_table_prefix: Create a CSV data table in the Theory folder to organize the current node's core objects, symbols, or concept comparisons. Columns: name, one_sentence_explanation, applicable_condition, typical_example, common_misconception, related_kc. Create only the CSV file; do not generate a full standard theory material.
node_page.complete_desc: To finish a node well, follow this learning flow:
node_page.complete_outline_detail: First have the AI generate a learning outline (blueprint); theory and practice materials are then generated from it to stay complete and on-track
node_page.complete_theory_detail: Understand core concepts and save materials to the Theory folder
node_page.complete_practice_detail: Do hands-on exercises and save notes to the Practice folder
node_page.complete_review_detail: Use the Feynman technique to verify real understanding
node_page.complete_personalize: AI-generated materials are a starting point, not the finish line: analyze and question them against your own needs, then use AI to enrich or rewrite them into a version that truly fits you.

dag_canvas.connect_self: A node cannot connect to itself

dag_canvas.connect_cycle: Cannot connect: this would create a cycle
dag_canvas.chapter_connect_mixed: Chapter links must connect chapter to chapter; node links must connect node to node
dag_canvas.chapter_connect_no_anchor: This chapter has no suitable node for creating a dependency
dag_canvas.edge_reconnect_invalid: Cannot reconnect: this would duplicate, self-link, or create a cycle
dag_canvas.prerequisite_cycle: Those prerequisites would create a cycle, so the save was cancelled
dag_canvas.delete_confirm: Delete "{{name}}"? Related connections will be removed too.
dag_canvas.drag_chapter: Drag the chapter header to move this chapter
dag_canvas.chapter_moved: Moved "{{chapter}}". Click save to keep it.

model.no_key_needed: This provider does not require an API key
model.key_stored_in_keychain: Key is stored in system Keychain — never written to any file
model.models_added_removed: {{added}} new model(s) added, {{removed}} old model(s) removed

model.models_empty_removed: No usable chat models were found. {{count}} old model(s) removed
model.add_provider_key_name_placeholder: Leave blank to use provider ID

learning.guidance_desc: Controls how the AI guides you while learning

search.intro: When configured, the AI will search the web for up-to-date content while generating study materials. Without keys it falls back to pure LLM generation.
search.tavily_desc: General web search, good for timely content (free tier: 1000/month). Get a key at app.tavily.com
search.exa_desc: Semantic neural search, good for authoritative docs, academic papers, and high-quality tutorials; complements Tavily (free tier: 1000/month). Get a key at exa.ai
search.youtube_desc: Auto-searches teaching videos for sports/music/art topics (free quota: 10000 units/day). Get a key in Google Cloud Console.

advanced.ocr_worker_desc: Controls how many local recognition workers run at the same time for scanned PDF OCR
advanced.ocr_worker_help: Default is 2. More workers can finish faster, but use more CPU and memory. Use 1-2 for large files or low memory; try 3-4 on stronger machines. Newly started OCR jobs use this setting.
advanced.search_budget_desc: Controls web learning-source depth, query count, page fetch count, and automatic import policy
advanced.search_budget_help: Queries control how many search intents are issued; pages control how many full web pages are fetched. Deeper search is usually more robust but slower and uses more search API budget. When community auto-import is off, personal/community posts stay low-priority candidates.
advanced.youtube_proxy_desc: Used when importing video URLs to fetch video metadata and subtitles
advanced.youtube_proxy_help: Leave blank to use HTTP_PROXY / HTTPS_PROXY / ALL_PROXY from the environment. The proxy is only passed to yt-dlp in the main process and does not affect normal chat model requests.
advanced.youtube_cookies_desc: Used for videos that require login, age verification, or account permissions
advanced.youtube_cookies_help: Only the browser type, profile name, or cookies.txt path is saved. Cookie contents are not stored and are never sent to AI. Log in to the relevant video site in that browser before using browser mode.
advanced.ytdlp_desc: Video URL import uses yt-dlp to fetch video metadata, manual subtitles, and automatic captions
advanced.ytdlp_missing_help: Install first downloads the matching binary from the official yt-dlp GitHub Release; if GitHub fails, it falls back to Python pip when available.
advanced.whisper_desc: On-device engine that transcribes audio/video to text. Apple Silicon (M-series) Macs only. Click to install (creates a Python venv and downloads mlx-whisper; requires internet).
advanced.whisper_ready_help: mlx-whisper is installed; on-device transcription is available.
advanced.whisper_missing_help: Not installed yet. Click the button to install (downloads several hundred MB).
advanced.whisper_install_failed: Local transcription install failed; check your network and retry.
advanced.ffmpeg_desc: Audio processing tool required by local transcription. Not bundled due to size and licensing — install it yourself.
advanced.ffmpeg_install_help: Install it yourself: on macOS run brew install ffmpeg, or download from ffmpeg.org. Restart the app afterwards to detect it.
advanced.storage_desc: Review local library, workspace, and cache usage, then clear cache/orphan files that do not affect saved sources
advanced.storage_help: Only Ulyzer-owned local folders are cleaned. Original files from Desktop, Downloads, and other user locations are never deleted. Clearing transcription runtime cache may make the next audio/video transcription initialize again.
advanced.storage_runtime_confirm: Clearing transcription runtime cache may make the next audio/video transcription reinitialize or download dependencies again. Continue?

about.description: A task-driven, resource-adaptive AI learning system.
about.ai_models: AI: Claude · GPT · Gemini · Grok · DeepSeek · Qwen · MiniMax · OpenRouter · Ollama
